Title: Novel JAZ co-operativity and unexpected JA dynamics underpin Arabidopsis defence responses to Pseudomonas syringae infection

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA mediates defense and hormone signaling; COR promotes pathogen virulence and chlorosis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of JAZ5 together with JAZ10 enhances COR-mediated chlorosis, modestly increases susceptibility to Pseudomonas syringae DC3000, and alters JA/SA/ABA dynamics.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ5 is a core repressor component that co-operates specifically with JAZ10 to restrict COR phytotoxicity during DC3000 infection. 2. The jaz5/10 double mutant, but not jaz5 alone or other double jaz combinations, showed strong chlorosis and increased bacterial growth. 3. JAZ5 induction during infection is partly COR dependent and partly COR-independent, consistent with additional bacterial/MAMP signals sustaining its expression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reverse genetics using T-DNA single/double/triple mutants; pathogen infection phenotyping; bacterial growth assays; hormone profiling; qRT-PCR time course; COR co-infiltration assays; root growth assays; mRNA-seq comparison of Col-0 vs jaz5/10
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by DC3000 by 6 hpi; maximal around 8 hpi; early induction also occurs with COR-deficient bacteria, indicating MAMP responsiveness; sustained expression requires additional bacterial signals and is partly COR dependent.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf infection context; induced during bacterial challenge, with early COR-independent and later partially COR/COI1-dependent expression.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ jasmonate ZIM-domain repressor protein; contains EAR domains; lacks the highly conserved LPIARR motif reported for many JAZ proteins
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 infection; COR co-infiltration; COR-deficient DC3000 mutants; leaf bacterial challenge
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes enhanced COR sensitivity/chlorosis and modestly enhanced pathogen susceptibility.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: JAZ5 was treated here as a core non-enzyme/non-TF gene. The decisive phenotype required combinatorial loss with JAZ10, indicating functional co-operativity rather than strong single-gene effect. COR alone induced only transient JAZ5 expression.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: CATMA microarray, GEO: GSE56094; mRNA-seq, GEO: GSE72461
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing JAZ5 function together with JAZ10 may reduce COR-mediated disease damage, but effects are context-dependent and tied to defense-hormone network balance.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA mediates defense and hormone signaling; COR promotes pathogen virulence and chlorosis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: JAZ10 collaborates with JAZ5 to suppress COR cytotoxicity, restrict pathogen growth, and modulate late JA accumulation during infection.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ10 is a decisive partner of JAZ5 in limiting COR-induced chlorosis during DC3000 infection. 2. jaz10 single mutants showed mild enhanced chlorosis and supported increased bacterial growth at high inoculum, but the strongest phenotype occurred in jaz5/10. 3. JAZ10 expression is strongly sustained during infection, largely dependent on COR and COI1, and the JAZ10.3 splice isoform accumulates from 8 hpi.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reverse genetics using jaz10 and jaz5/10 mutants; infection phenotype scoring; bacterial growth assays; hormone profiling; qRT-PCR including splice-variant analysis; COR co-infiltration assays; mRNA-seq
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapid induction by DC3000 around 6 hpi, with sustained accumulation peaking around 16 hpi; early induction occurs without COR, but later expression is strongly COR and COI1 dependent; JAZ10.3 splice variant accumulates strongly from 8 hpi.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf infection context with strong time-dependent induction during DC3000 challenge.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ jasmonate ZIM-domain repressor protein; alternatively spliced; JAZ10.3 isoform predicted to retain COI1 binding but resist efficient ubiquitination
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 infection; COR treatment; COR-deficient bacterial mutants; coi1-16 background
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss contributes to increased disease symptoms and pathogen growth, especially in combination with jaz5.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Sustained JAZ10 accumulation during infection is largely attributable to JAZ10.3, a splice form predicted to attenuate JA signaling by resisting ubiquitin-mediated degradation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: CATMA microarray, GEO: GSE56094; mRNA-seq, GEO: GSE72461
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating JAZ10, particularly in combination with JAZ5 or specific splice forms, may help tune resistance to COR-producing pathogens, but excessive repression of JA pathways may have defense tradeoffs.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: oxylipin phytohormone; phenolic phytohormone; phytotoxin hormone mimic
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: JA and SA coordinate defense signaling; COR mimics JA-Ile to promote bacterial virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: COI1 is required for much of COR-responsive JAZ10 expression and influences infection-associated JA and SA dynamics, but a substantial portion of late JA accumulation during COR-deficient infection is COI1 independent.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. coi1-16 altered hormone dynamics during infection, showing that COR-dependent suppression of SA is not fully COI1 dependent and that late JA accumulation can occur substantially without COI1. 2. COI1 was required for nearly all JAZ10 and about half of JAZ5 transcript accumulation during infection. 3. The study uses COI1 as a key mechanistic comparator for COR/JAZ signaling behavior.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis using coi1-16; targeted hormone profiling; qRT-PCR time-course of JAZ5/JAZ10; pathogen infection assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional receptor assayed genetically in infected leaves; not primarily evaluated by expression profiling.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box jasmonate receptor component of SCF ubiquitin ligase complex
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation compromises most jasmonate responses and alters defense hormone balance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as core because receptor genetics were central to interpretation of hormone dynamics and JAZ induction in results, though the main paper emphasis was on JAZ5/JAZ10 co-operativity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning COI1-dependent signaling may alter disease outcomes, but broad disruption likely incurs major jasmonate-response liabilities.
